steps.title: Identify defensible evidence
steps.content: The sentence says some music may have an infinite number of possible meanings. In that context, an interpretation is difficult to call incorrect when the listener's supporting evidence has a sound, fair, or defensible basis.

steps.title: Test each meaning
steps.content: Choice A, inferior, would make the evidence weaker. Choice B, identical, means exactly the same. Choice C, repetitive, means repeated. None explains why an interpretation would be difficult to reject.

steps.title: Choose the valid quality
steps.content: Choice D, reasonable, describes evidence that can fairly support an interpretation. If the evidence is reasonable, it is hard to justify saying the interpretation is incorrect. The answer is D.
